particulars_of_right_to_be_exercised_or_.indicate_which_right_is_to_be_exercised_: Right of access to and correction of personal information held by a private body, and the right to fair labour practices and non-discriminatory treatment in employment, including proper calculation and payment of contractual and statutory benefits.
particulars_of_right_to_be_exercised_or_.explain_why_record_is_required_for_exerc:
I am requesting access to my full employment and payroll records from my former employer, Brightwave Digital (Pty) Ltd, in order to exercise and protect several interrelated rights, primarily my right to fair labour practices, my right to access and, if necessary, correct personal information held about me, and my right to pursue an accurate and lawful calculation of monies that may still be owed to me.

By way of background, I, Thabo Sipho Maseko, was employed by Brightwave Digital (Pty) Ltd as a Senior Account Manager from 2019-03-01 until my resignation on 2024-08-31. During my exit process, I noticed a number of discrepancies relating to my final payslip, outstanding leave payout, and the calculation of my performance bonus for the 2023/2024 financial year. Specifically:

1. **Final salary and leave payout discrepancies**
   - My final payslip for August 2024 reflects payment for only 8 days of accumulated annual leave, whereas my own records (including screenshots from the internal HR portal taken on 2024-07-15 and again on 2024-08-05) indicate that I had 14.5 days of accrued leave at the time my resignation became effective.
   - There is also an unexplained deduction labelled “Benefits Adjustment” in the amount of R3,250.00 on the August 2024 payslip. No explanation was provided to me despite two email requests (dated 2024-09-03 and 2024-09-10) to the HR department.

2. **Performance bonus calculation and eligibility**
   - My employment contract (signed on 2019-02-20) states that I am eligible for an annual performance-related bonus of up to 15% of my cost-to-company package, pro-rated based on performance and tenure during the applicable financial year.
   - In previous years, I received bonuses that were calculated on the basis of both individual KPIs and team performance. For the 2023/2024 period, my line manager verbally confirmed in our year-end review meeting on 2024-05-28 that I met or exceeded most of my KPIs, and that I should expect a bonus in line with prior years, subject to final approval from senior management.
   - However, no bonus was paid to me in June or July 2024 (the usual bonus months at the company), and I have never received any written explanation as to why. I was informally told by a colleague that management had reclassified certain client revenue I generated, which may have affected the bonus pool, but I have never been given any official documentation or breakdown.

3. **Right of access to and correction of personal information**
   - I have a right to access the personal and employment information that the company holds about me, including performance reviews, disciplinary records (if any), correspondence related to my performance and remuneration, and any internal notes that may have affected decisions about my bonus and final payments.
   - I am concerned that there may be inaccurate or incomplete information on file that has negatively impacted my bonus eligibility and/or leave calculations. For example, in 2022 I changed teams within the company, and there was some confusion at the time about who was responsible for updating my KPIs and sales targets in the system. If those updates were not made correctly, it may appear on paper that I underperformed when in reality I met the targets agreed upon verbally with my then-new manager.

Because of the above, I am requesting access to the following records, which I believe are held by the private body and are necessary for me to exercise and protect my rights:

- All payslips issued to me from 2023-03-01 to 2024-08-31, inclusive.
- A complete breakdown of my annual leave accrual and usage from 2019-03-01 to 2024-08-31, including any adjustments made by HR or payroll, with dates, reasons, and authorising officials.
- The detailed calculation of my final salary and benefits for August 2024, including an explanation and supporting documentation for the “Benefits Adjustment” deduction of R3,250.00.
- All documents and internal communications directly related to the calculation (or non-calculation) of my performance bonus for the 2023/2024 financial year, including but not limited to:
  - My performance appraisal forms and KPI scorecards for 2023 and 2024.
  - Any emails, memos, or internal notes between my line manager, HR, and senior management concerning my bonus eligibility or the adjustment of client revenue associated with my accounts.
  - Any spreadsheets, reports, or system extracts used to calculate the overall bonus pool and my individual allocation for that period.
- My signed employment contract and any subsequent addenda or amendments, especially those dealing with remuneration, commission/bonus structures, and leave entitlement.
- Any internal HR or payroll policies that were applied in determining my final pay, leave payout, and bonus eligibility for the 2023/2024 period.

These records are necessary because, without them, I am unable to:

1. **Verify the correctness of payments and identify potential underpayment**
   I need to check whether the company has complied with its contractual obligations and any applicable labour laws in respect of my remuneration. If there has been an underpayment of my final salary, leave payout, or bonus, I intend to seek rectification. This may involve first engaging with the company informally, and, if that fails, considering a formal dispute resolution process through the CCMA or another appropriate forum. I cannot make an informed or fair decision about whether to proceed without seeing the underlying calculations and supporting records.

2. **Correct any inaccurate or incomplete personal information**
   If the records reveal that incorrect KPIs, targets, or performance ratings were used, I will need to request correction of that information, as such inaccuracies may not only have affected my bonus but could also impact references or future background checks where the company is asked to comment on my performance. I have a legitimate interest in ensuring that the information they hold about me is accurate, complete, and not misleading.

3. **Exercise my right to fair labour practices**
   The right to fair labour practices includes being remunerated correctly for work done and benefits earned, and being assessed on the basis of accurate, transparent criteria. Access to the requested records will allow me to determine whether I have been treated fairly relative to colleagues in similar roles, and whether any decisions made about my pay or bonus were arbitrary, discriminatory, or inconsistent with company policy.

4. **Prepare for possible legal or administrative proceedings**
   Depending on what the records show, I may need to:
   - Prepare a detailed written grievance to the company;
   - Lodge a complaint or referral with the CCMA or another regulatory body; and/or
   - Consult with an attorney about a potential claim for outstanding remuneration or damages.
   In all of these scenarios, I will need documentary evidence of how my pay, leave, and bonus were calculated, as my recollection and personal notes will not be sufficient on their own.

To illustrate the practical impact this has had on me:
- The shortfall I suspect in my final payout (once I compare my own calculations with the company’s figures) could amount to between R10,000 and R25,000. This is a significant sum for me, especially as I was unemployed for two months after leaving Brightwave Digital and had to rely on my savings and credit card to cover rent and living expenses.
- I am currently in the process of applying for a home loan, and the bank has asked for proof of income and employment history. Any inaccuracies or unexplained deductions in my final payslips could raise questions or delay the approval of the loan.

In summary, the records I am requesting are directly and materially relevant to:
- Confirming whether my constitutional and statutory rights to fair labour practices and correct remuneration have been upheld;
- Enabling me to correct any inaccurate personal or performance information held by the company; and
- Allowing me to decide, on an informed basis, whether further steps are necessary to recover any monies owed or to challenge any unfair treatment.

Without access to these records, I am effectively operating in the dark and cannot properly exercise or protect these rights. That is why I respectfully request full and transparent access in the forms indicated in this application (both printed and electronic copies, if possible), so that I can review, verify, and, if needed, challenge the decisions that have been made in relation to my employment and remuneration.
